title: SARM ScopeItem
description: One atomic attestation task: a single Certifier answering a single question about a single Subject. Per §3.3. Schema URI: urn:ietf:params:sarm:schemas:core:1.0:ScopeItem

schemas.description: MUST contain the ScopeItem core schema URI as the first entry. Subsequent entries identify extension schemas.

id.description: Unique identifier for this ScopeItem within the Source System. Opaque token per §3.1.1.

version.description: HTTP ETag value for conditional requests per §4.5.

location.description: URI at which this ScopeItem can be retrieved individually per §4.5.

subjectId.description: Identifier of the subject of the question in the Source System. Opaque token per §3.1.1.

subjectLabel.description: Human-readable label for the subject, for presentation to the certifier.

certifierHint.description: Suggested certifier (external id). The Attestation Engine MAY override certifier resolution. Opaque token per §3.1.1.

decisionOptions.description: Ordered, non-empty list of the decisions the Source System will accept for this item. Owned entirely by the Source System per §3.3.1.

value.description: Machine-readable decision key returned to the Source System in a Decision.

label.description: Human-readable text for presenting this option to the certifier.

resourceId.description: Opaque grouping key. ScopeItems sharing a resourceId MAY be presented together by the Attestation Engine. Equivalence key only; never returned in a Decision. Omit when the item does not belong to a group. Per §3.1.1.

resourceLabel.description: Human-readable label for the grouping, for presentation only. Meaningful only when resourceId is present.

decisionPrompt.description: Source-System-defined prompt to present alongside the decision options. The Attestation Engine MAY override or supply its own.

contextData.description: Additional metadata to display to the certifier (last access time, role description, justification, etc.). Source Systems MAY include arbitrary keys. For human display only, not protocol behavior.
